Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Reopen Discovery And $1,488.00 In Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Arthur Meirson
Off Calendar Nov-24-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: DEFENDANT MARK DAVIS, BUDGET RENT A CAR SYSTEM, INC. Mtn To Reopen Discovery And $1,488.00 In Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Arthur Meirson is granted. ORDER SIGNED. Judge Pro Tem: Robert Kane; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Reclassify Case As A Limited Civil Case (Walker Motion)
Off Calendar Nov-10-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS MARK DAVIS and BUDGET RENT A CAR SYSTEM, INC.'S Motion To Reclassify Case As A Limited Civil Case (Walker Motion) - The motion is denied. Assuming that the property damage claim has settled, which is not entirely clear, defendant still fails to demonstrate that plaintiff's claim will necessarily result in a verdict of less than $25,000. Defendant merely relies upon the $413 doctor's bill and the arbitration award. Plaintiff declares that his quadriceps have a "grotesque scar" and his finger is "grotesquely disfigured" and he is having follow up care with a hand specialist. Without further information, the court cannot find that plaintiff will be unable to obtain an award exceeding $25,000. The court denies the request for sanctions. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg
